- Define failure states and retry/escalation semantics.
- Define how failures are reported to immediate parent versus root/top-level agent.
- Define how failure events appear in dashboard runtime overlay and audit log.
- Define how loop exhaustion interacts with escalation/fallback nodes.

title: Add Kanban-style epic task board with dependency visibility
description:
Create a clearer planning/execution view for large epics such as pi-agent-orchestrator-0403 before starting the workflow orchestration epic in earnest.

Problem:
- Large epics currently list tracer bullets, but the intended order, current focus, blockers, and dependency context are hard to scan.
- Agents/operators need an obvious read-only view of what is ready, blocked, in progress, focused, and done.
- Users want a better UI for visualizing 20-30 issue epics without turning Roadmap into the orchestration engine.

Goal:
- Provide a Kanban-style dashboard view for an epic's related tasks, backed by Seeds issues, explicit epic membership, dependency data, and Seeds extension fields for focus/order.
- Make it easy to see what is ready, blocked, in progress, current focus, backlog/open, and done.
- Make dependency/blocker relationships visible without abusing Seeds dependency edges for non-hard relationships.

Clarified v1 direction:
- Roadmap remains a visualization/planning surface, not a work launcher.
- Do not add Roadmap Start Work, agent spawn, handoff, or workflow execution controls.
- No Review/Validate column; review/validation should be represented as normal explicit Seeds issues with open/in_progress/closed status.
- No drag/drop mutation in v1. The AI agent/orchestrator workflow or Seeds tools update issue state; Roadmap reflects changes.
- Use Seeds extension fields for Current Focus and optional ordering metadata.
- Dependency visualization can be a simple read-only tree/list/Gantt-like sequence or generated Mermaid preview, not a complex graph editor.
- Epic membership uses explicit `Part of <epic-id>` references rather than dependency edges or inferred label/title grouping.
- Expanded board can use a horizontally scrollable area or a wide/full-window-style modal to give large epics enough space.

Tracer bullets / suggested order:
1. pi-agent-orchestrator-1eca — Replace inferred Roadmap epic grouping with explicit membership. (done)
2. pi-agent-orchestrator-a7e1 — Define Epic Kanban board columns and card semantics.
3. pi-agent-orchestrator-0aab — Add Roadmap epic board DTO and bucketing view model.
4. pi-agent-orchestrator-bb78 — Render Kanban board in expanded Roadmap epic detail.
5. pi-agent-orchestrator-1420 — Surface hard dependency tree and blockers in Epic Kanban board.
6. pi-agent-orchestrator-a382 — Read Seeds extension fields for Epic Kanban focus and ordering.

Deferred/removed from Roadmap v1:
- pi-agent-orchestrator-aa7e — Drag-and-drop transitions; not needed because Roadmap should not mutate task workflow state in v1.
- pi-agent-orchestrator-f515 — Agent next-task spawn/handoff affordances; work launch belongs to the orchestrator/workflow engine.

Dependency intent:
- a7e1 defines semantics before DTO/focus-order work.
- 0aab provides the board model used by UI and dependency visibility.
- bb78 renders the board.
- 1420 layers dependency visualization on the board model.
- a382 layers Seeds extension-field focus/order display after semantics are defined.
- Dependency edges are hard implementation blockers only; epic membership remains explicit `Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05` text.

Relationship to pi-agent-orchestrator-0403:
- Intended as pre-work to make the Workflow Orchestration epic easier to execute and inspect.
- Do not add a hard dependency edge unless we decide pi-agent-orchestrator-0403 truly must not proceed until this board exists.

Acceptance:
- Define how an epic task board discovers member issues without misusing dependency edges.
- Define board columns and which issue fields/metadata each column maps to.
- Show hard dependencies/blockers clearly on cards and in a dependency panel/tree/preview.
- Provide a clear visual ordering/focus/readiness view for agents/operators.
- Support pi-agent-orchestrator-0403 as an example epic with its tracer bullets.
- Preserve Seeds dependency semantics: blocks/blockedBy are hard dependencies only.

title: Replace inferred Roadmap epic grouping with explicit membership
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Replace the current Roadmap epic grouping heuristic with explicit membership so unrelated issues are not pulled under new epics by shared labels or title tokens.

Problem:
- The current Roadmap view infers epic children from dependency links, shared distinctive labels, and title tokens.
- This caused a new Kanban/planning epic to show unrelated active/closed children and blocker counts because it shared labels like workflow/seeds/dashboard.
- Seeds dependencies must remain hard blockers only, not epic membership.

Preferred direction:
- Use explicit membership markers, starting with the existing convention: issue description contains `Part of <epic-id>`.
- Optionally support a future dedicated membership metadata field or label convention, but do not rely on broad shared labels or title affinity.
- Show dependency links/blockers in dependency panels or card badges, not as child membership.

Acceptance:
- Roadmap epic children are derived from explicit `Part of <epic-id>` membership only, or another deliberate first-class membership marker if chosen.
- Remove/fence off broad label/title inferred grouping for epic children.
- Do not treat hard dependency edges as epic children.
- Existing epics such as pi-agent-orchestrator-0403 still show their tracer bullets through explicit `Part of` references.
- New epics with broad labels do not accidentally inherit unrelated tasks.
- Add tests covering explicit membership, dependency-only non-membership, and shared-label non-membership.
closeReason: Implemented explicit Roadmap epic membership via `Part of <epic-id>` descriptions, removed dependency/label/title inferred grouping, updated panel/view-model tests, and validated with `bun run check`.

title: Define Epic Kanban board columns and card semantics
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Define the v1 Kanban-style board semantics for an expanded Roadmap epic.

Decisions clarified:
- No Review/Validate column in Roadmap v1. Review/validation should be represented as normal explicit Seeds issues with open/in_progress/closed status.
- Roadmap is a visualization/planning surface only; it should not start work, spawn agents, or directly drive orchestration.
- Work starts through the orchestrator/workflow engine, not through Roadmap buttons.
- Dependency edges remain hard blockers only, not epic membership or ordering.

V1 columns:
- Backlog/Open: open epic children that are not blocked, not current focus, and not in progress.
- Ready: open epic children with no unresolved blockers, highlighted as actionable/available.
- Current Focus: open or in_progress epic children marked through Seeds extension metadata as the current operator/agent focus.
- In Progress: issues with Seeds status `in_progress`.
- Blocked: non-closed issues with unresolved hard blockers.
- Done: closed issues.

Scope:
- Define exact column precedence when a card qualifies for multiple columns, especially Current Focus vs In Progress vs Blocked.
- Define which Seeds fields, readiness checks, and Seeds extension metadata map cards into each column.
- Define card content: issue id, title, status, priority, labels, blocker/dependent counts, and compact acceptance/progress hints.
- Define how hard dependency blockers differ from epic membership and manual ordering/focus.
- Define visualization-only transitions/state explanations; do not define Roadmap start-work or drag/drop mutation behavior.

Acceptance:
- Document v1 column definitions and card fields.
- Define how Ready and Blocked are computed from Seeds dependency state.
- Define how Current Focus differs from in_progress status and is read from Seeds extension metadata.
- Define computed ordering rules and optional manual ordering metadata.
- Explicitly exclude Review/Validate, Roadmap Start Work, drag/drop mutation, and agent spawn/handoff from v1 Roadmap scope.
- Preserve Seeds dependency semantics: dependency edges are hard blockers only, not epic membership or ordering.

title: Add Roadmap epic board DTO and bucketing view model
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Add a first-class view model/DTO for rendering a Kanban board for a single explicit Roadmap epic.

Scope:
- Build from explicit epic membership (`Part of <epic-id>`) and existing Seeds issue/dependency data.
- Bucket child issues into board columns based on the column semantics from pi-agent-orchestrator-a7e1.
- Include card metadata needed by the dashboard: issue status, priority, labels, blockers, dependents, readiness, and focus/order metadata if available.
- Keep hard blockers visible as dependency metadata, not child membership.

Acceptance:
- Export a typed EpicBoard/RoadmapEpicBoard DTO or view model.
- Add tests for explicit epic membership, ready/blocked/in-progress/done bucketing, and dependency metadata.
- Existing Roadmap hierarchy behavior remains intact.
- No broad label/title inference is reintroduced.

title: Render Kanban board in expanded Roadmap epic detail
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Render the Kanban-style board when a user expands or opens an epic in the Project Roadmap dashboard.

Decisions clarified:
- Preserve the current Project Roadmap overview.
- The Kanban board should appear from epic expansion/detail, preferably with enough space for 20-30 issues.
- A wider modal or expanded full-window-style dialog is acceptable and may be better than squeezing many columns into the inline roadmap row.
- Roadmap is visualization/planning only; do not add Start Work, spawn/handoff, or orchestration controls.

Scope:
- Add board columns inside an expanded epic detail experience, either inline with horizontal scrolling or in a wide/full-window modal opened from the epic expansion.
- Cards should be compact but readable, with issue id copy/open affordances and status/priority/blocker badges.
- Empty columns should explain what belongs there.
- The board should work for large epics such as pi-agent-orchestrator-0403.

Acceptance:
- Expanded epic view or wide epic board modal shows the Kanban board columns from the board DTO.
- Existing Roadmap overview and issue detail modal/navigation remain usable.
- Cards can open the existing issue detail dialog and copy issue ids.
- No Roadmap Start Work, spawn, or handoff actions are introduced.
- Empty/loading/error states are handled.
- Tests cover rendering columns and issue cards for a representative large epic.

title: Surface hard dependency tree and blockers in Epic Kanban board
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Make hard dependencies and blockers easy to understand from the epic Kanban board.

Decisions clarified:
- Do not build a complex graph editor in v1.
- A simple dependency tree, compact Gantt-like sequence, or generated Mermaid preview is enough for visualizing blockers/order.
- Dependency visualization is read-only in Roadmap. Creating/changing dependency edges remains a separate Seeds/orchestrator action.

Scope:
- Show unresolved blocker counts and resolved blocker counts on cards.
- Provide a simple dependency tree/list or Mermaid-style preview for the selected epic/card.
- Highlight why a card is in Blocked and which issues must close first.
- Show dependencies that point outside the current epic without treating them as epic children.
- Avoid creating or changing dependency edges from this view.

Acceptance:
- Blocked cards clearly list unresolved blockers.
- Cards with dependents show compact dependent counts/details.
- External blockers/dependents are visibly marked as outside the current epic.
- A simple read-only dependency tree/list/Mermaid preview helps visualize blocker relationships.
- Dependency-only linked issues do not appear as children unless they also have explicit `Part of <epic-id>` membership.
- Tests cover blocker detail rendering and external dependency display.

title: Read Seeds extension fields for Epic Kanban focus and ordering
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Define and implement read-side support for user/agent focus and optional ordering in the epic Kanban board using Seeds extension fields.

Decisions clarified:
- Use Seeds extension fields for ordering and focus metadata.
- Roadmap should visualize focus/order metadata, not provide drag/drop mutation controls in v1.
- The AI agent/orchestrator workflow can update Seeds state as part of normal work; Roadmap reflects the result.
- Dependency edges must not be used for ordering or focus.

Scope:
- Define the Seeds extension field shape for epic board focus and optional manual order.
- Read current-focus membership and optional manual order per epic.
- Surface focus/order in the board DTO and UI.
- Ensure metadata is resilient when issues close, reopen, or leave an epic.

Acceptance:
- Current Focus can be represented independently from issue status through Seeds extension fields.
- Optional manual card order can be read from Seeds extension fields, or a deliberate v1 non-goal is documented.
- Roadmap refresh/reload preserves focus/order display from Seeds data.
- No drag/drop or Roadmap mutation API is required for v1.
- Tests cover focus/order read and reload behavior.

title: Add drag-and-drop transitions for Epic Kanban cards
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Add lightweight drag-and-drop or equivalent move controls for cards in the expanded epic Kanban board.

Scope:
- Support moving cards into allowed columns based on the transition rules from pi-agent-orchestrator-a7e1.
- Moving to In Progress should update Seeds status to `in_progress`.
- Moving to Done should close or prompt for close reason if needed.
- Moving to Current Focus should update focus metadata from pi-agent-orchestrator-a382 without necessarily changing status.
- Moving blocked cards into Ready/In Progress should be rejected unless blockers are resolved.
- Provide accessible non-drag controls for keyboard users.

Acceptance:
- Cards can be moved between allowed columns.
- Invalid moves show a clear reason and do not mutate state.
- Mutations use existing Roadmap/Seeds APIs rather than direct file edits.
- The board refreshes after successful moves while preserving the selected epic.
- Tests cover allowed moves, rejected blocked moves, and accessible fallback controls.
closeReason: Deferred/removed from Roadmap v1 scope after clarification. Roadmap should visualize large epics, not mutate task state via drag/drop; work/status changes should be driven through the orchestrator/workflow path or Seeds tools.

title: Add agent next-task affordances to Epic Kanban board
description:
Part of pi-agent-orchestrator-ea05.

Make the epic Kanban board useful for agent-driven work selection.

Scope:
- Highlight the next ready task(s) for an agent based on priority, blockers, current focus, and manual order.
- Provide actions such as Copy issue id, Start work, Focus this, and optionally Spawn/hand off to agent when orchestration mode supports it.
- Show enough context for an agent/operator to understand why a task is next.
- Avoid starting work on blocked tasks unless explicitly overridden with blocker context.

Acceptance:
- Board has a clear `next ready` affordance for the current epic.
- Cards explain readiness/blocking/focus state in compact form.
- `Start work` continues to update Seeds status safely.
- Optional agent-spawn/handoff actions are gated behind existing orchestration capabilities and can be deferred if not available.
- Tests cover next-task selection and start-work affordances.
closeReason: Deferred/removed from Roadmap v1 scope after clarification. Roadmap should not start work, spawn agents, or hand off tasks; those actions belong to the orchestrator/workflow engine. Keep Roadmap focused on visualization.
